reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [Use of unsafe function 'gets' which is deprecated and may cause buffer overflow; however, the immediate error is that when expansion occurs, the code skips the character after '-', leading to missing characters or incorrect output. Also, after expanding, it does not copy the ending character of the range (e.g., for a-d, 'd' is never appended).]

17.reason: [Incorrect index update: should be 'j += n' instead of 'j = j + n - 1'; this skips one character and causes missing output]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [After the loop, n equals (a[i+1]-a[i-1]), so j becomes j + (a[i+1]-a[i-1]) - 1, which skips the last character of the expansion. Should be j += n;]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [After the loop, 'n' equals 'a[i+1]-a[i-1]', so 'j+n-1' skips one character too few; should be 'j += n' or 'j = j + n']
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [Incorrect index update: should be 'j += n' instead of 'j = j + n - 1'; this skips one character and causes missing output]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.2 , reason:  [should be j += n, because after loop n = a[i+1]-a[i-1], so j becomes j + (a[i+1]-a[i-1]) - 1, which skips one character; correct would be j += n or j = j + n]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [This should be 'j = j + n;' because the loop runs 'n = a[i+1]-a[i-1]' times, producing characters from a[i-1]+1 up to a[i+1]. But then we skip incrementing 'i' correctly and also fail to include the final character a[i+1] in some interpretations. However, more critically, after expansion, the next character (a[i+1]) must not be processed again, but the current logic increments i by only 1, so the next iteration will process a[i+1] as a normal char, causing duplication. Also, digits are not handled at all per problem requirements.]

18.reason: [This skips the next character after '-', but if expansion occurs, we should skip both '-' and the next char (i+=2), otherwise the next char will be processed again.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This skips the next character after '-', but if expansion occurs, we should skip both '-' and the next char (i+=2), otherwise the next char will be processed again.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Skips the next character after '-', but doesn't skip the character after that, leading to duplication or misprocessing]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.2 , reason:  [only increments i by 1, but should skip the next character (a[i+1]) since it's already expanded; should be i += 2 to avoid reprocessing the end character]

8.reason: [duplicate #include <stdio.h> is harmless but unnecessary; however, the real issue is missing bounds check for a[i+1] leading to out-of-bounds read when '-' is at end]

13.reason: [missing check that i+1 is within bounds (i.e., a[i+1] != '\0'), causing undefined behavior when '-' is last character; also fails to handle digit ranges [0-9]]
